title: Fixing Trie Prefix Search Edge Case

statement.markdown: Write a function to check if a given string is a prefix of any string in the trie. The function should return True if the string is a prefix of any string in the trie, False otherwise. Assume the trie is already constructed and implemented.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a list of strings and a query string. The query string will be a prefix to search in the trie.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a boolean value indicating whether the query string is a prefix of any string in the trie.
statement.notes: The trie is a prefix trie, where each node can have multiple children corresponding to different characters.

examples.input:
# Sample input
test
testcase
test
query
examples.explanation: The query string 'test' is a prefix of the strings 'testcase' and 'test'.

hints.text: Check the code implementation of the TriePrefixSearch function.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Trie Prefix Search Edge Case
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to traverse the trie based on the query string and return True if any node is a match. Otherwise, return False.
editorial.bruteForce: Traverse the trie for each query string and for each string in the trie to check if the query string is a prefix.
editorial.optimalSolution: Traverse the trie based on the query string and return True if any node is a match.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This approach ensures that we correctly identify the query string as a prefix of any string in the trie.
editorial.dryRun: Use a sample trie with strings and query strings to demonstrate the optimal solution.
commonMistakes: forgetting to check if a node is a match

functionSignature.python: def TriePrefixSearch(self, trie: list[list[int]], query: str) -> bool:

testCases.input:
# Sample input
test
testcase
test
query
